name: TitleMax Title Secured Loans
description_short: TitleMax provides title-secured loans and personal loans with same-day cash approval at physical locations and online. Most credit types accepted.
description_long:
TitleMax is a title loan and personal loan lender with multiple physical store locations, including the Rock Hill, SC branch at 1671 North Cherry Rd. The company specializes in providing fast cash solutions to consumers who need emergency funds quickly, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional credit-based lending.

The company offers two primary products: (1) Title-secured loans, where borrowers use their vehicle title as collateral to borrow cash while keeping their car, and (2) Personal loans for unexpected or emergent financial needs. Title loans can be initiated online with vehicle appraisal via smartphone photos, then completed in-store. Personal loans require a valid government ID and bank statement or proof of income from the past 60 days. Both products emphasize quick approval and same-day cash availability.

TitleMax differentiates itself by accepting most credit types and focusing on vehicle value rather than credit scores for title loans. The company highlights competitive rates, quick approval processes, and the ability to keep driving while financing a title loan. The Rock Hill location displays strong customer reviews (5.0/5 average on Google with 564+ reviews), with repeated praise for staff member Cassandra and service quality. The store is bilingual and maintains extended hours (10am-7pm most weekdays, Saturday hours).

However, title-secured loans carry inherent risks: failure to repay could result in vehicle repossession, which is not mentioned in the marketing materials. While the website emphasizes competitive rates, specific APR ranges are not disclosed, making cost comparison difficult. Personal loans require active bank accounts with recent deposits or income verification, excluding unbanked consumers. The company conducts credit checks despite marketing to those with poor credit, creating uncertainty about actual approval odds.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Same-day cash available upon approval for title loans
Online application and vehicle appraisal process available via smartphone
Most credit types accepted; credit score not primary qualification
Keep your vehicle while using title as collateral
Bilingual staff at Rock Hill location
Extended weekday hours (10am-7pm) and Saturday availability
5.0/5 Google rating with 564+ customer reviews praising service
cons: Vehicle repossession risk if unable to repay title loan; not clearly disclosed in marketing
Specific APR ranges not provided on website; competitive rates claimed but unverified
Personal loans require active bank account with recurring deposits or recent paystubs, excluding unbanked consumers
Credit checks performed despite marketing to poor-credit borrowers, creating approval uncertainty
best_for: Vehicle owners needing emergency cash within 24 hours with poor or no credit history
Consumers who prefer in-person service and staff interaction over online-only lenders
Bilingual Spanish speakers seeking same-day lending in the Rock Hill, SC area
diagnosis: TitleMax is best for vehicle owners with poor credit or thin credit files who need emergency cash within 24 hours and can afford to risk their car as collateral. The main caveat is that title loans carry high repossession risk if payments are missed, and the company does not disclose APRs, making it difficult to assess true cost relative to other emergency lenders or payday alternatives.
services: Title-secured loans using vehicle as collateral
Online title loan application and vehicle appraisal
In-store document signing and cash pickup
Personal loan applications with quick approval
Vehicle appraisal based on submitted photos
Credit checks for both loan types
Online loan portal access for account management
In-store and online personal loan management
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
